sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.60 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let$V$be an infinite countable elementary abelian additive 2-group. Does${ Aut}\, V$contain a subgroup$G$such that [r]{(a)}$G$is transitive on the set of non-zero elements of$V$, and [r]{(b)} if$H$is the stabilizer in$G$of a non-zero element$v V$, then$V= v V_v$, where$V_v$is$H$-invariant,$H$is isomorphic to the multiplicative group$P^*$of a locally finite field$P$of characteristic$2$, and the act...
